science.title: Ancient teeth reveal clues to the environment humans’ early ancestors evolved in millions of years ago
science.description: Through fossilized tooth enamel, scientists are reconstructing the diets and landscapes that existed millions of years ago. We really are what we eat.

science.title: A “lost world” beneath the North Sea was once full of forests
science.description: Long before rising seas swallowed Doggerland beneath the North Sea, this lost landscape may have been a surprisingly lush and life-friendly haven. New DNA evidence reveals that forests of oak, elm, and hazel were already thriving there more than 16,000 years ago—thousands of years earlier than scientists thought possible. Even more astonishing, researchers detected traces of a tree species believed to have vanished from the region hundreds of thousands of years ago.

science.title: Scientists warn of 3,100 “surging glaciers” that can trigger floods and avalanches
science.description: A hidden threat is emerging in the world’s glaciers: while most are shrinking, a rare group known as “surging glaciers” can suddenly accelerate, unleashing powerful and sometimes destructive events. Scientists have identified over 3,100 of these glaciers worldwide, with many clustered in high-risk regions like the Arctic and the Karakoram Mountains, where communities lie directly in their path.

science.title: 6,000 Cubic Kilometers of Magma Hiding Beneath Tuscany Revealed with New Ambient Noise Tomography Technique
science.description: Beneath Tuscany lies 6,000 cubic kilometers of hot magma, according to new European research using a remote-sensing technique.

science.title: Nobody Thought a Material Could Beat Copper at Heat Transfer. This One Just Did by a Landslide
science.description: Scientists have identified a metallic material that shatters a century-old record, and copper may never be the same again.

science.title: Some planets with water are still uninhabitable, scientists find
science.description: Scientists find that planets need more than water to support life. Too little water may cause long-term overheating and instability.

science.title: A New Way To Send Messages Inside the Body
science.description: A cellular courier system carries active biomolecules across barriers, enabling safer, more effective RNA and gene therapies.
